description: Students implement 2D discrete convolution — first with for-loops, then vectorized via im2patches and matrix multiplication. Mathematical convolution (kernel flipped), not cross-correlation. Pure 2D tensors (no batch/channel dims).

conv2d_loops.description: 2D convolution using for-loops. Must flip kernel (mathematical conv, not xcorr).

tests.name: loops_delta
tests.check:
assert result.shape == (8, 8), f'Wrong shape: {result.shape}'
assert torch.allclose(result, expected, atol=1e-5), f'Delta test failed, max diff {(result - expected).abs().max():.6f}'
tests.description: Convolving with delta should return the (cropped) image

tests.name: loops_correctness
tests.check:
assert result.shape == expected.shape, f'Shape {result.shape} vs {expected.shape}'
assert torch.allclose(result, expected, atol=1e-4), f'Max diff {(result - expected).abs().max():.6f}'
tests.description: Correctness against reference on random input

tests.name: loops_nonsquare
tests.check:
assert result.shape == expected.shape, f'Shape {result.shape} vs {expected.shape}'
assert torch.allclose(result, expected, atol=1e-4), f'Max diff {(result - expected).abs().max():.6f}'

tests.name: loops_is_conv_not_xcorr
tests.description: Verifies this is convolution (flipped kernel), not cross-correlation

im2patches.description: Extract all (kH, kW) patches from a 2D image into a matrix of shape (H_out*W_out, kH*kW). No unfold allowed.

tests.name: patches_correctness
tests.check: assert torch.allclose(result, expected, atol=1e-5), f'Max diff {(result - expected).abs().max():.6f}'

tests.name: patches_nonsquare
tests.check:
assert result.shape == expected.shape, f'Shape {result.shape} vs {expected.shape}'
assert torch.allclose(result, expected, atol=1e-5), f'Max diff {(result - expected).abs().max():.6f}'

tests.name: patches_first_patch
tests.check: assert torch.allclose(result[0], expected), 'First patch should be top-left 3x3 block flattened'
tests.description: First patch is the top-left block, correctly flattened

conv2d_vectorized.description: 2D convolution using im2patches + matrix multiplication. Must flip kernel.

tests.name: vec_correctness
tests.check:
assert result.shape == expected.shape, f'Shape {result.shape} vs {expected.shape}'
assert torch.allclose(result, expected, atol=1e-4), f'Max diff {(result - expected).abs().max():.6f}'

tests.name: vec_matches_loops
tests.check: assert torch.allclose(result, expected, atol=1e-4), f'Vectorized and loop outputs differ, max diff {(result - expected).abs().max():.6f}'
tests.description: Vectorized output must match the student's own loop output

tests.name: vec_is_conv_not_xcorr

common_mistakes.symptom: loops_is_conv_not_xcorr or vec_is_conv_not_xcorr fails with 'cross-correlation' message
common_mistakes.diagnosis: Student did not flip the kernel before sliding. They implemented cross-correlation instead of convolution.
common_mistakes.typical_deduction: Full points for the affected function

common_mistakes.diagnosis: Delta is symmetric so flipping doesn't matter. The student likely forgot to flip the kernel — check if the xcorr test also fails.
Student probably used H-kH or H-kH+2 instead of H-kH+1 for valid padding.

common_mistakes.symptom: vec_matches_loops fails but vec_correctness passes (or vice versa)
common_mistakes.diagnosis: One of the two implementations has a bug. Compare both to the reference to identify which.
common_mistakes.typical_deduction: Points for the buggy function only

common_mistakes.diagnosis: Check for import errors, syntax errors, or runtime crashes in student code. Often a technical issue unrelated to understanding.
common_mistakes.typical_deduction: Grade what you can; give partial credit for correct-looking code that crashed for technical reasons

grading_notes.partial_credit_policy: If the approach is clearly correct but has a minor bug (off-by-one, wrong axis), give 60-80% of the function's points. If the approach is fundamentally wrong (no kernel flip, wrong formula), give 0-20%. If code crashes for technical reasons but the logic looks sound, give 40-60%.
